steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that paired leaves should tend to show opposite side orientation. Supporting data must confirm this pattern holds across the species tested — all three grapevine species.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The number of leaf pairs showing opposite side orientation is fairly high in the July grape, but not as high as it is in the graybark grape.: only compares two species and says nothing about the frost grape. Incomplete support. ✗
  • Choice B — Although the number of leaf pairs showing same side orientation is fairly high in the July grape, it is much lower in both the frost grape and graybark grape.: highlights same-side orientation, which is the opposite of what the hypothesis predicts. ✗
  • Choice C — Although the exact ratio varies by species, the graybark grape, frost grape, and July grape all show more leaf pairs with opposite side orientations than with same-side orientations.: confirms the predicted pattern (opposite > same) across all three species tested. ✓
  • Choice D — In the graybark grape, frost grape, and July grape, all the leaf pairs show opposite-side orientation.: claims 100% prevalence, which the graph does not show and is implausibly absolute. ✗
That matches Choice C — Although the exact ratio varies by species, the graybark grape, frost grape, and July grape all show more leaf pairs with opposite side orientations than with same-side orientations..
